mechanism_narrative: A persistent upward trend in FEDFUNDS raises the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, compressing valuation multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter as long as the rate continues rising.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS 3-month trend and 60-day forward returns stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or returns are not significantly negative when FEDFUNDS trend is positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS month-over-month growth decelerates below its trailing 6-month average by more than 50%, it signals an inflection in labor demand that precedes broader earnings weakness, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS MoM growth falls below 50% of its trailing 6-month average growth rate
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are positive or flat in more than 55% of triggered deceleration episodes over the sample, or IC < 0.02 in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: The joint condition of elevated FEDFUNDS combined with decelerating INDPRO indicates tightening monetary policy is beginning to bite real economic output, and this combination predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next month more strongly than either series alone.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 4% AND INDPRO 3-month change turns negative
failure_condition: The interaction term (FEDFUNDS level x INDPRO decline) fails to add incremental predictive power over either univariate signal (delta Rank IC < 0.01) across walk-forward folds.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion regime, sustained positive month-over-month growth in RSAFS signals resilient consumer spending that supports corporate revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next month, but this relationship weakens or reverses once the cycle turns.
failure_condition: RSAFS trend shows no significant positive correlation with forward 20-day returns (Rank IC < 0.03) specifically during periods classified as late-cycle expansion using UNRATE and FEDFUNDS context.

mechanism_narrative: A UNRATE level sustained below 4% reflects a tight labor market consistent with strong aggregate demand, generating modest positive S&P 500 returns over the following week through continued consumption and earnings support.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive mean when UNRATE < 4% versus the unconditional sample mean (t-test p > 0.10) across the walk-forward sample.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive months of declining INDPRO signals an industrial production contraction that historically precedes broader recession risk pricing, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter once activated.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day forward returns in more than 60% of triggered 2-month INDPRO decline episodes across the historical sample.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) are highly rate-sensitive; when FEDFUNDS is elevated and HOUST simultaneously trends downward, the combined signal indicates a housing-led demand contraction that predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter beyond what either series predicts alone.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 4% AND HOUST 3-month change is negative
failure_condition: The joint HOUST-FEDFUNDS interaction signal fails to outperform a univariate HOUST-only model by more than 0.01 Rank IC across rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: During a regime where markets anticipate a Federal Reserve pause or pivot, a decelerating CPIAUCSL month-over-month change signals disinflation that supports expectations of future rate cuts, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no significant positive association with CPIAUCSL MoM deceleration (Rank IC < 0.02) during periods identified as pivot-anticipation regimes.
